reasoning: The election took place on March 15, 2026. News reports from that day confirm Denis Sassou Nguesso cast his vote and that an internet outage was implemented during the election, which is a common tactic used by authoritarian regimes to control information and ensure their continued grip on power. Given that Sassou Nguesso has been deeply entrenched in power for over four decades, has previously changed the constitution to maintain his position, and has imprisoned key opposition members, his victory is practically a foregone conclusion. His active participation on election day removes the remote uncertainty of him withdrawing or passing away prior to the vote. Thus, the probability of him being declared the winner is exceptionally high.
